_comment: NPC: Merchant Hong (洪商人). INFERRED — Great Wang Capital inhabitant. Canon ID: INF-GWC-04. Per Article II (Reality First): the Zhao Country capital must have inhabitants. These NPCs are inferred from the city's district structure (governor mansion, temple, market, tavern, port). Prime Directive: these beings exist independently of the player's perception.
name: Merchant Hong
personality: shrewd, opportunistic, well-informed; knows every price in the capital
relationship_to_wanglin: INFERRED — neutral; sees everyone as a potential customer

perception_tiers.foundation: Merchant Hong — Mortal (non-cultivator). INFERRED capital resident.
perception_tiers.nascent_soul: Merchant Hong — minor figure in the capital's power structure

note: INFERRED from Great Wang Capital district structure. shrewd, opportunistic, well-informed; knows every price in the capital

dao_heart.note: 0-100. A cracked Dao heart blocks breakthrough regardless of Qi.

initiation_lines: Ah, a customer! Welcome to the Great Wang Capital market. I have goods from every province — iron from the northern mines, wheat from the southern farmlands, and rare items from the port.
The port docks see ships from distant lands. Well, distant provinces at least. If you need something specific, ask — I can get almost anything. For a price.
The Teng family's merchants get the best stalls. Those of us without cultivation backing make do with what remains. But I have connections. Do not underestimate a mortal with a network.
Gold and emeralds talk in this city. If you have materials to sell, I will give you fair prices. Fairer than the Teng family's buyers, at least.

sect_tasks.description: Merchant Hong rubs his hands: 'I have a buyer in the cultivator quarter who needs iron and emeralds. Bring me the materials and I will pay you in gold — minus my commission, of course.'

Merchant Hong whispers: 'A cultivator in the quarter is willing to pay well for mundane herbs with alchemical potential. Wheat, carrots, and coal for the furnace. Bring me these and I will handle the rest.'
